steps.title: Learn the function OT-2 quickly
steps.instruction: Learn the function OT-2 quickly! This simple plating protocol shows the flexibility of custom protocols hosted on our Protocol Library. Reagents are transferred down columns and then across rows as shown here. If the source labware is selected to be a 24x tuberack, reagents will be transferred in quadrupliate (ex: reagent tube A1 to destination labware wells A1-D1, reagent tube B1 to destination labware wells E1-H1, reagent tube C1 to destination labware wells A2-D2, etc.) If the source labware is selected to be a 12-channel reservoir, reagents will be transferred column to column (ex: reservoir channel 1 to destination labware column 1, reservoir channel 2 to destination labware column 2, etc.) ---

steps.title: Reagents are transferred sequentially from the...
steps.instruction: Reagents are transferred sequentially from the source labware to the corresponding positions of the destination labware.

steps.title: If you are using the magnetic...
steps.instruction: If you are using the magnetic module, the module engages 18mm.

steps.title: The protocol finishes execution and prompts...
steps.instruction: The protocol finishes execution and prompts the user for next steps.
